What's going everyone? I wanted to add a video here because it needed a little bit more clarification for people and so that is what I'm doing here. So the section before this where it said download the app, that is where they would probably be picking their workout times etc. And so I want to clarify some things that you're going to want to make sure happen at this point in the process. So first we always explain an 80-20 soft commitment. This is if you do not do cancellation fees for your workout which I highly recommend you start implementing. But if you do not have no show fees for planning a workout then you explain to them, listen I need you to pick a time which is kind of the second piece here. I need to pick a time that's going to work best. So the first thing you're going to do is pick the time for them that's best for you. So you're going to pick your least packed time which is like probably your 7 a.m. Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday right? You're going to pick that first and then you're going to go backwards in reverse order of what's most convenient for you. Okay so you'll be like how about this time and the selling point here is that I want to pick I want these classes for you because they're going to be the ones we're going to get the most personalized attention right? And so you're going to sell it on that benefit and that way you can smooth out wherever you're going to showing up so that you don't have everyone just say they want to come to the 530. Your 530 is packed but you're 4 o'clock empty and a lot of people would do that if you just told them that's when you want them to come. And a lot of people prefer to have more personalized service so you can sell that benefit by picking the times that are most empty. Alright so don't think of it as like them doing you a favor you're giving them better service in those instances. So first pick the time that's least convenient for you and then select Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday since that's usually the time where people are less likely to they're less like like most people are going to want Monday, Wednesday, Friday and so if you start with Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday then half the people are just going to say okay to that and I really think twice about it. If you say Monday, Wednesday, Friday, first everyone will say yes and then you'll have empty you know Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturdays. Alright if you're not doing the once you've picked these times if they get really anal about it like well my schedule changes blah blah blah that's when you'll explain to them the 80-20 soft commitment. So I just want to be clear there I said it a little bit out of order but hopefully that makes sense. So pick the time then do Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday then do Monday, Wednesday, Friday and then if you're not doing a fee explain that you're like listen 80-20 soft commitment means 80% of the time that's when you're gonna come and if for some reason you can't make it just let us know but we can always guarantee that if you come at that time we'll be able to work with you and the class sessions will be overpacked and you're gonna be able to get in and get a great work on it. Okay. Now after that point make sure they know exactly how to sign up for classes and they know exactly when they're coming back next. So at this point they should know when their next work out is going to be and I like asking them like cool when's your next workout, when's the next time you're coming in and then they're like Tuesday at seven perfect who's gonna be your trainer and then they would tell me uh John I'd be like cool so you're gonna see John when Tuesday at seven okay great and so like I would drill them on this because so they remember so they're not just like glossing their eyes as they're going through this all right I'm being very clear here and if the more direction you take with them the more respect you and then the more likely they will be to buy later on in this because you're taking command and you've shown expertise I'm being really clear here like the way you do this matters. So at that point they should know where their accountability coach is and who their trainer will be on that first day because people just want to know what happens next and as long as you fulfill that promise they will trust you so much more if you always do what you say you're gonna do. Okay so next one on your end on the back end the trainer should know who the next clients are going who the new clients are going to be at each time slot so if you know that Susie's coming seven on Tuesdays Thursdays and Saturdays then at seven o'clock on Tuesday that trainer John should have the list of the people who were signed up for those slots and should introduce those people to the class make a special note to meet them greet them by name uh and show that you are expecting them all right like those details make a big difference and whether or not people come back that first week that next workout all right it's important and then the last piece is um if you are going to be implementing new processes the best thing about having external plays is that all these new customers don't know what you used to do and so if you want to say yep this is how we do it they're not going to know any different right and so if you want to change a culture in your gym the best thing to do is start with these new customers so if you want to start giving them key cards and getting them to check in with key cards then do it if you want them to start you want to start a no-show fee of $15 then do it with these existing customers and then you can you can phase it into your existing members as as time goes on but like the best thing is when you have a big wave of new people coming in is these are the opportunities you have to like implement some of the systems that you probably didn't have in the beginning all right so this is step 1.5 this is just the double check to make sure that they know and they feel 100 comfortable what happens next before you get into the nutrition
